name: French Onion Soup (Marguerite Patten)
recipeIngredient: 15 fluid ounces beef stock or water with 3/4 beef stock cube
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the onions into thin rings.
2. Halve the rings so they are easier to eat.
3. Crush the garlic clove.
4. Heat the margarine in a saucepan.
5. Add the onions and garlic to the pan and cook steadily for about 10 minutes, or until golden in color.
6. Stir well to prevent burning.
7. Add the flour, blend with the onions, then pour in the stock or water.
8. Bring the liquid to a boil, add the beef stock cube (if using) along with a little seasoning and sugar.
9. Cover the pan and simmer gently for 20 to 25 minutes, or until the onions are tender.
10. Spoon into individual soup bowls.
11. Top with bread or toast and grated cheese.
12. Serve immediately or place under the grill for 1 to 2 minutes only to melt the cheese. Do not leave for too long, otherwise the cheese becomes stringy.
